message.content:
Design the Storybook development strategy for Rolling Stock Mileage Management frontend.

## Context
- No backend exists yet - all development via Storybook with mock data
- PatternFly design system components
- 9 major screens with ~50+ unique components
- Must support visual regression testing with Playwright

## Requirements

### Storybook Setup
- Storybook 8 with Vite
- PatternFly theme integration
- Viewport addon for responsive testing
- A11y addon for accessibility
- Controls addon for props manipulation
- Docs addon for documentation

### Component Categories (Atomic Design)
1. **Atoms**: Status chips, badges, progress bars, icons
2. **Molecules**: KPI cards, alert items, filter pills, train cards
3. **Organisms**: Data tables, chart panels, notification center
4. **Templates**: Dashboard layout, grid layout, form layout
5. **Pages**: Full screen compositions

### Mock Data Strategy
- Create realistic mock data for 128 trains
- Mock mileage history (144 data points per train)
- Mock maintenance schedules
- Mock alerts and notifications
- Use MSW (Mock Service Worker) for API mocking

### Stories to Create (Priority)
1. KPICard (4 variants: availability, compliance, utilization, cost)
2. FleetStatusPanel (with donut chart)
3. TrainMileageGrid (128 rows, virtual scroll)
4. ManHourChart (area + line overlay)
5. AlertCard (4 severity levels)
6. TrainCard (for mobile view)
7. FilterToolbar (fleet, status, PM due)

### Visual Testing
- Chromatic or Percy for visual regression
- Playwright for E2E visual tests
- Screenshot comparisons across breakpoints

Design:
1. Storybook folder structure
2. Story naming conventions
3. Mock data fixtures
4. Decorator patterns for PatternFly theme
5. Viewport configurations for responsive
6. Accessibility testing approach

Provide detailed Storybook architecture with example story structures.

   223вҶ’
   224вҶ’### IBM Maximo Insights:
   225вҶ’- Role-based dashboards (Fleet Manager, Technicians, Planners, Dispatchers)
   226вҶ’- Fleet Usage Load: Spreadsheet-like UI for bulk meter reading entry
   227вҶ’- Dynamic fleet grouping by Asset Attributes
   228вҶ’- February 2025 update emphasizes role-based mobility

   231вҶ’### Rolling Stock Solutions Analyzed:
   232вҶ’- **voestalpine zentrak**: Real-time dashboard with asset condition overview
   233вҶ’- **eeedo**: Barrier-free info between drivers, operators, management, maintenance
   234вҶ’- **Toshiba RSMS**: Mileage/running records linked to shunting plans
   235вҶ’- **DELMIA Rail Planning**: Virtual Twin for predictive maintenance
   236вҶ’Source: https://eee.do/rolling-stock-maintenance-software/

   238вҶ’### Resource Leveling Chart Best Practices (CRITICAL for RFQ Section 8):

   241вҶ’- **Gap Visualization**: "Without adjustment" vs "With adjustment" comparison
   242вҶ’- **Key Metrics**: Utilization rates, overallocation alerts, capacity gaps
   243вҶ’Source: https://thedigitalprojectmanager.com/projects/managing-schedules/resource-planning-visualization/
   244вҶ’
   245вҶ’### Key Design Decisions:

   289вҶ’## MILEAGE-UX-002 [COMPLETE] - Man-Hour Projection Chart Design
   290вҶ’
   291вҶ’### Created Files:
   292вҶ’1. docs/ux/man-hour-projection-chart.md - Full UX specification document
   293вҶ’2. src/mileage/types/man-hour-chart.ts - TypeScript types for chart
   294вҶ’
   295вҶ’### Key Design Decisions (from RFQ Section 8 Analysis):
   296вҶ’
   297вҶ’**Chart Type:** Stacked Area/Line Combination Chart
   298вҶ’- Three data series: Availability, Adjusted Workload, Predicted Workload
   299вҶ’- Monthly granularity with 12-year (144 month) support
   300вҶ’- Gap analysis with color-coded severity (green/yellow/orange/red)
   301вҶ’
   302вҶ’**Data Table (Below Chart):**
   303вҶ’- Timeline | No of Task | Maint. Work | Adj. Workload | Avail(MH) | Manpower | Gap(MH)
   304вҶ’- Gap row with color highlighting per RFQ Section 8
   305вҶ’
   306вҶ’**Gap Severity Thresholds:**
   307вҶ’- Gap > 500: Comfortable (green)
   308вҶ’- 0 < Gap <= 500: Near capacity (yellow)
   309вҶ’- -500 <= Gap < 0: Slight overallocation (orange)
   310вҶ’- Gap < -500: Critical overallocation (red)
   311вҶ’
   312вҶ’**Comparison Modes:**
   313вҶ’- Without Adjustment: Shows predicted workload (unleveled)
   314вҶ’- With Adjustment: Shows leveled workload (optimized)
   315вҶ’- Side-by-Side: Shows both charts stacked
   316вҶ’
   317вҶ’**Timeline Controls:**
   318вҶ’- Date pickers for start/end
   319вҶ’- Quick ranges: 1M, 3M, 6M, 1Y, 3Y, ALL
   320вҶ’- Arrow navigation for shifting view
   321вҶ’
   322вҶ’**Responsive Design:**
   323вҶ’- Desktop (>=1200px): Full chart + table
   324вҶ’- Tablet (768-1199px): Horizontal scroll table
   325вҶ’- Mobile (<768px): Card-based view
   326вҶ’
   327вҶ’### Unknown Unknowns Discovered:
   328вҶ’1. Timezone issues (man-hours span midnight)
   329вҶ’2. Holiday calendars (availability varies by country)
   330вҶ’3. Overtime calculation (gap doesn't account for OT)
   331вҶ’4. Skill mismatch (man-hours != qualified hours)
   332вҶ’5. Multi-depot planning (different capacity per depot)
   333вҶ’6. Emergency maintenance (unplanned work disrupts schedule)
   334вҶ’
   335вҶ’### TypeScript Types Created:
   336вҶ’- ManHourDataPoint, ChartPeriod, GapSeverity
   337вҶ’- ManHourChartConfig, ManHourChartState
   338вҶ’- ChartTooltipData, ManHourSummary
   339вҶ’- Helper functions: classifyGap, getGapColor, calculateSummary
   340вҶ’

   343вҶ’- Nielsen Norman Group: Chart type selection
   344вҶ’- Runn.io: Workload management tools
   345вҶ’- UX Magazine: Data visualization handbook

   988вҶ’## MILEAGE-UX-009 [COMPLETE] - Alert/Notification System UI
   989вҶ’
   990вҶ’### Created Files:
   991вҶ’1. docs/ux/alert-notification-system.md - Full 18-section UX specification
   992вҶ’2. src/mileage/types/notification-ui.ts - Comprehensive TypeScript types
   993вҶ’
   994вҶ’### Research Sources:
   995вҶ’- Smashing Magazine: Design Guidelines For Better Notifications UX
   996вҶ’- Toptal: Comprehensive Guide to Notification Design
   997вҶ’- MagicBell: Notification System Design
   998вҶ’- Carbon Design System: Notification Pattern (IBM)
   999вҶ’- Fleetio: Fleet Management Notifications
  1000вҶ’- Pitstop: Alert Management Workflows in Fleet Operations
  1001вҶ’
  1002вҶ’### Key Design Decisions:
  1003вҶ’
  1004вҶ’**1. Priority Levels (P1-P4):**
  1005вҶ’- P1 Critical: Red (#DC2626), immediate response, 15-min auto-escalate
  1006вҶ’- P2 Warning: Amber (#F59E0B), 4-hour response, 24-hour escalate
  1007вҶ’- P3 Info: Blue (#3B82F6), 24-hour response, no escalation
  1008вҶ’- P4 Success: Green (#22C55E), no response required
  1009вҶ’
  1010вҶ’**2. Alert Categories:**
  1011вҶ’- Maintenance: PM overdue/critical/approaching, inspections, fault codes
  1012вҶ’- Mileage (RFQ Section 5): Overrun/underrun alerts, fleet imbalance
  1013вҶ’- Assignment: No suitable train, conflicts, schedule not published
  1014вҶ’- Resource: Man-hour shortage, track overbooked
  1015вҶ’- Cost: Budget exceeded/warning, variance
  1016вҶ’- System: Integration failures, sync delays
  1017вҶ’
  1018вҶ’**3. Notification Types:**
  1019вҶ’- Toast: Transient, top-right, 5s auto-dismiss, max 3 stacked
  1020вҶ’- Inline: Contextual page alerts, dismissible
  1021вҶ’- Actionable: Require user decision, up to 3 CTAs
  1022вҶ’- Modal: Blocking critical decisions
  1023вҶ’
  1024вҶ’**4. Notification Center:**
  1025вҶ’- Bell icon with badge count (pulse for critical)
  1026вҶ’- Tabs: All, Unread, Critical, Actions
  1027вҶ’- Grouped by date (Today, Yesterday, etc.)
  1028вҶ’- Quick actions: Schedule PM, View Details, Acknowledge
  1029вҶ’
  1030вҶ’**5. Delivery Channels:**
  1031вҶ’- In-App: All alert types
  1032вҶ’- Push: P1, P2, most P3 (configurable)
  1033вҶ’- Email: All types + daily digest option
  1034вҶ’- SMS: P1 critical only (optional)
  1035вҶ’
  1036вҶ’**6. Escalation Workflow (4 tiers):**
  1037вҶ’- Tier 1: Assigned user, 15 min wait
  1038вҶ’- Tier 2: Supervisor, 30 min wait
  1039вҶ’- Tier 3: Management, 1 hour wait
  1040вҶ’- Tier 4: Executive, phone call option
  1041вҶ’
  1042вҶ’**7. User Preferences:**
  1043вҶ’- Channel toggles (In-App/Push/Email/SMS)
  1044вҶ’- Quiet hours with critical override
  1045вҶ’- Daily digest with configurable time
  1046вҶ’- Category-specific channel preferences
  1047вҶ’- Frequency limits with bundling
  1048вҶ’
  1049вҶ’**8. Snooze Options:**
  1050вҶ’- 15 minutes, 1 hour, 4 hours
  1051вҶ’- Tomorrow morning (9 AM)
  1052вҶ’- Custom duration
  1053вҶ’- P1 alerts cannot be snoozed
  1054вҶ’

  1056вҶ’- AlertPriority, AlertPriorityConfig, AlertCategory, AlertCategoryConfig
  1057вҶ’- 6 alert type unions (Maintenance, Mileage, Assignment, Resource, Cost, System)

  1102вҶ’### Key Design Decisions:
  1103вҶ’
  1104вҶ’**1. Responsive Breakpoints:**
  1105вҶ’- xs (<576px): Mobile, single column
  1106вҶ’- sm (576-767px): Mobile large, single column
  1107вҶ’- md (768-991px): Tablet portrait, 2 columns
  1108вҶ’- lg (992-1199px): Tablet landscape, 3 columns
  1109вҶ’- xl (>=1200px): Desktop, full layout
  1110вҶ’
  1111вҶ’**2. Navigation Patterns:**
  1112вҶ’- Desktop: Horizontal top navigation bar
  1113вҶ’- Tablet: Condensed tabs + hamburger for secondary
  1114вҶ’- Mobile: Bottom tab bar (5 tabs max) + slide-out menu
  1115вҶ’
  1116вҶ’**3. Card-Based Layout (Mobile):**
  1117вҶ’- Replace data tables with cards on mobile
  1118вҶ’- Collapsible sections for complex data
  1119вҶ’- Horizontal scroll for essential tables
  1120вҶ’
  1121вҶ’**4. Touch Interactions:**
  1122вҶ’- Minimum 48x48px touch targets
  1123вҶ’- Swipe gestures (left/right for actions)
  1124вҶ’- Pull-to-refresh, pinch-to-zoom
  1125вҶ’
  1126вҶ’**5. Offline-First Design:**
  1127вҶ’- App shell architecture (cached shell, dynamic content)
  1128вҶ’- Service worker caching strategies
  1129вҶ’- Optimistic UI updates with background sync
  1130вҶ’- Offline indicators and sync status
  1131вҶ’
  1132вҶ’**6. Field Worker Optimizations:**
  1133вҶ’- High contrast mode for outdoor glare
  1134вҶ’- Large touch targets (64px) for gloves
  1135вҶ’- Voice input support
  1136вҶ’- QR code scanning for train check-in
  1137вҶ’
  1138вҶ’**7. Role-Based Mobile Views:**
  1139вҶ’- Operations: Schedule-focused, assignment management
  1140вҶ’- Maintenance: Task list, check-in flow, inspections
  1141вҶ’- Management: KPIs, approvals, escalations
  1142вҶ’
  1143вҶ’**8. PWA Features:**
  1144вҶ’- Web app manifest for installation
  1145вҶ’- Service worker for offline support
  1146вҶ’- Background sync for queued actions
  1147вҶ’- Push notifications

  1216вҶ’Claude Code CLI supports:
  1217вҶ’- `-p, --print` for non-interactive mode (useful for pipes)
  1218вҶ’- `--output-format json|stream-json` for structured output
  1219вҶ’- `--system-prompt <prompt>` for system prompts
  1220вҶ’- `--model <model>` for model selection (opus, sonnet)
  1221вҶ’- `--input-format text|stream-json` for input
  1222вҶ’
  1223вҶ’Command format:
  1224вҶ’```bash
  1225вҶ’claude -p --output-format json --system-prompt "system" "prompt"
  1226вҶ’```

# LLM Harness Progress Log
# Created: 2026-01-07
# Pattern: Anthropic Harness for long-running agents

## Purpose
This file persists state between Claude sessions.
Read this FIRST at session start to understand context.

## Project Overview
- Local LLM orchestration for M2 Pro 16GB
- Uses Ollama for local models + Claude API for complex tasks
- Router pattern: phi4-mini classifies вҶ’ delegates to appropriate model

## Stack
- Runtime: Bun.js + TypeScript
- Local: Ollama (phi4-mini, deepseek-r1:8b, qwen-coder)
- Cloud: Claude Opus 4.5 via Anthropic SDK
- State: @belt/tools (state machine)
- Hooks: @belt/ai (cascade rules)

============================================================

[2026-01-07T00:00:01Z] Session #1 [COMPLETED] Initial project setup

============================================================

[2026-01-07] Downloaded phi4-mini-reasoning (3.2GB) - router model
[2026-01-07] Downloaded qwen2.5-coder:7b (4.7GB) - code tasks
[2026-01-07] Configured Ollama environment variables for M2 Pro

============================================================

[2026-01-07T23:08:41.408Z] Session #3 [STARTED] Session started

[2026-01-07] Report contains: MILP formulation, competitor analysis, implementation strategy
[2026-01-07] Session #3 [COMPLETED] Technical report complete

============================================================
SESSION #4 STARTED - 2026-01-07
[2026-01-07] Session #4 [STARTED] Rail Maintenance Scheduling Implementation

## Completed Research:
- Railway Maintenance Scheduling MILP - academic papers
- Possession Management terminology
- Competitor UX analysis (Prometheus GWOS, IVU.rail, ZEDAS, GIRO, Signature Rail)
- Column Generation & OR-Tools integration

## Key Discoveries (Unknown Unknowns):
- What-If Scenario Planning: Competitors lack robust implementation (differentiator)
- Offline-First Mobile: Critical for remote rail locations, competitors weak
- Constraint Explanation: Show WHY schedule is infeasible (competitors hide this)
- Two Distinct UIs: Field Worker (mobile) vs Planner (desktop) - one-size-fits-all fails

## Implementation Progress:
[2026-01-07] Updated package.json: added highs@1.8.0, vitest@1.2.0
[2026-01-07] Created src/scheduling/types/ directory with:
  - maintenance-event.ts: MaintenanceEvent, EventType, LinearLocation
  - resource.ts: LaborRequirement, EquipmentRequirement, ResourcePool
  - constraint.ts: HardConstraint, SoftConstraint, ConstraintSet
  - possession.ts: Possession, PossessionWindow, TimeHorizon
  - schedule.ts: Schedule, LeveledSchedule, SchedulingResult
  - network.ts: Block, TrackSection, NetworkTopology
  - index.ts: Re-exports all types

## Solver Module Created:
[2026-01-07] Created src/scheduling/solver/ directory with:
  - highs-wrapper.ts: HiGHS WASM solver integration
  - lp-builder.ts: CPLEX LP format builder with scheduling helpers
  - greedy-fallback.ts: Heuristic fallback scheduler
  - solution-parser.ts: Parse solver results into Schedule objects
  - index.ts: Module exports

## Engine Module Created:
[2026-01-07] Created src/scheduling/engine/ directory with:
  - scheduling-engine.ts: Main orchestration (MILP + fallback + validation)
  - demand-analyzer.ts: Resource demand analysis for leveling
  - constraint-validator.ts: Hard/soft constraint validation
  - local-improver.ts: Post-optimization local search
  - index.ts: Module exports

## Agents Module Created:
[2026-01-07] Created src/scheduling/agents/ directory with:
  - leveling-agent.ts: Resource leveling optimization agent
  - index.ts: Module exports

## E2E Tests Created (NO MOCKS - Real Solver):
[2026-01-07] Created tests/scheduling/ directory with:
  - fixtures/test-data.ts: Test scenarios and helpers
  - solver.test.ts: HiGHS solver integration tests
  - engine.test.ts: Full scheduling pipeline tests
  - leveling-agent.test.ts: Leveling agent tests
[2026-01-07] Created vitest.config.ts

## Harness Integration Complete:
[2026-01-07] Added SCHEDULING category to router (src/harness/router.ts)
[2026-01-07] Added schedulingAgent to agents (src/agents/index.ts)
[2026-01-07] Added TaskCategory 'SCHEDULING' to types (src/types/index.ts)
[2026-01-07] Exported scheduling module from main index (src/index.ts)
[2026-01-07] Fixed type errors and ran full test suite

## E2E Tests Results (REAL HiGHS Solver - NO MOCKS):
[2026-01-07] All 36 tests pass:
  - solver.test.ts: 11 tests (HiGHS WASM, LPBuilder, GreedyScheduler)
  - engine.test.ts: 14 tests (Scheduling, Precedence, Resources, Fallback)
  - leveling-agent.test.ts: 11 tests (Leveling, Metrics, Edge Cases, Config)

## Session #4 [COMPLETED] - Rail Maintenance Scheduling System
- Types: MaintenanceEvent, Possession, Resource, Constraint, Schedule
- Solver: HiGHS WASM wrapper, LP builder, Greedy fallback
- Engine: Scheduling orchestration, Demand analysis, Constraint validation
- Agents: LevelingAgent for resource leveling optimization
- Tests: 36 E2E tests with real solver

## Plan File:

============================================================

[2026-01-08] Session #5 [STARTED] Rolling Stock Mileage Management System

## NEW PROJECT - RFQ Based
- RFQ Source: ~/RFQ.pdf
- 128 trains: 49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom
- 12-year contract period
- Three categories: Maintenance Planning, Operational Planning, Resource Planning

## Implementation Progress:

  - Train, FleetType, FitnessStatus, TrainStatus, PMLevel

  - Default thresholds and helper functions

[2026-01-08] MILE-003 [COMPLETE] Maintenance plan types (maintenance-plan.ts)
  - MaintenancePlan, ScheduledMaintenance, Overhaul
  - SparePart, MaintenanceActivity, MaintenanceIssue
  - Default plans for Kinki Sharyo and Alstom fleets

[2026-01-08] Git commit: e805c9e - MILE-001, MILE-002, MILE-003 complete

[2026-01-08] MILE-004 [COMPLETE] Run assignment types (run-assignment.ts)
  - Route, RunAssignment, DailySchedule, TrainRecommendation
  - AssignmentOptimizationRequest/Result, MileageDeviation
  - Helper functions for train scoring and deviation status

[2026-01-08] MILE-005 [COMPLETE] Resource and cost types (resource.ts)
  - ManHourAllocation, ManHourConfig, WorkshopTrack, TrackSlot
  - CostBaseline, CostProjection, CostCompliance
  - LongTermResourcePlan, AnnualResourcePlan (12-year horizon)
  - Default configs and helper functions

[2026-01-08] MILE-006 [COMPLETE] Simulation types (simulation.ts)
  - MileageStrategy, Scenario, ScenarioParameter

  - ScenarioComparison, SensitivityAnalysis, MultiScenarioAnalysis

- **Key Metrics**: Utilization rates, overallocation alerts, capacity gaps

3. Resource histogram as primary visualization (per RFQ case study)

## MILEAGE-UX-002 [COMPLETE] - Man-Hour Projection Chart Design

### Created Files:
1. docs/ux/man-hour-projection-chart.md - Full UX specification document
2. src/mileage/types/man-hour-chart.ts - TypeScript types for chart

### Key Design Decisions (from RFQ Section 8 Analysis):

**Chart Type:** Stacked Area/Line Combination Chart
- Three data series: Availability, Adjusted Workload, Predicted Workload
- Monthly granularity with 12-year (144 month) support
- Gap analysis with color-coded severity (green/yellow/orange/red)

**Data Table (Below Chart):**
- Timeline | No of Task | Maint. Work | Adj. Workload | Avail(MH) | Manpower | Gap(MH)
- Gap row with color highlighting per RFQ Section 8

**Gap Severity Thresholds:**
- Gap > 500: Comfortable (green)
- 0 < Gap <= 500: Near capacity (yellow)
- -500 <= Gap < 0: Slight overallocation (orange)
- Gap < -500: Critical overallocation (red)

**Comparison Modes:**
- Without Adjustment: Shows predicted workload (unleveled)
- With Adjustment: Shows leveled workload (optimized)
- Side-by-Side: Shows both charts stacked

**Timeline Controls:**
- Date pickers for start/end
- Quick ranges: 1M, 3M, 6M, 1Y, 3Y, ALL
- Arrow navigation for shifting view

**Responsive Design:**
- Desktop (>=1200px): Full chart + table
- Tablet (768-1199px): Horizontal scroll table
- Mobile (<768px): Card-based view

### Unknown Unknowns Discovered:
1. Timezone issues (man-hours span midnight)
2. Holiday calendars (availability varies by country)
3. Overtime calculation (gap doesn't account for OT)
4. Skill mismatch (man-hours != qualified hours)
5. Multi-depot planning (different capacity per depot)
6. Emergency maintenance (unplanned work disrupts schedule)

### TypeScript Types Created:
- ManHourDataPoint, ChartPeriod, GapSeverity
- ManHourChartConfig, ManHourChartState
- ChartTooltipData, ManHourSummary
- Helper functions: classifyGap, getGapColor, calculateSummary

### Sources Referenced:
- ProjectManager.com: Resource Histogram best practices
- Nielsen Norman Group: Chart type selection
- Runn.io: Workload management tools
- UX Magazine: Data visualization handbook

12. **applySchedule**: Save optimization result
13. **publishSchedule**: Publish schedule (make it active)
14. **calculateMileageDeviation**: Calculate deviation for train
15. **getFleetDeviations**: Get fleet-wide deviations
16. **getUnderrunTrains/getOverrunTrains**: Trains with deviation issues
17. **getFleetDailySummary**: Get fleet summary for date

### Key Features:
- Greedy optimization algorithm for train-route matching
- Score-based recommendations (fitness + mileage balance + PM distance)
- Fleet restriction and fitness requirement filtering
- Mileage deviation tracking (Вұ10% threshold for underrun/overrun)
- Reserve train support, exclude/include constraints
- Warning generation (PM approaching, insufficient trains, fitness issues)

### Optimization Weights:
- Fitness: 30% (prefer FIT over RESTRICTED)
- Mileage Balance: 40% (prefer lower mileage trains)
- PM Distance: 30% (prefer trains far from PM)

## MILEAGE-UX-009 [COMPLETE] - Alert/Notification System UI

### Created Files:
1. docs/ux/alert-notification-system.md - Full 18-section UX specification
2. src/mileage/types/notification-ui.ts - Comprehensive TypeScript types

### Research Sources:
- Smashing Magazine: Design Guidelines For Better Notifications UX
- Toptal: Comprehensive Guide to Notification Design
- MagicBell: Notification System Design
- Carbon Design System: Notification Pattern (IBM)
- Fleetio: Fleet Management Notifications
- Pitstop: Alert Management Workflows in Fleet Operations

### Key Design Decisions:

**1. Priority Levels (P1-P4):**
- P1 Critical: Red (#DC2626), immediate response, 15-min auto-escalate
- P2 Warning: Amber (#F59E0B), 4-hour response, 24-hour escalate
- P3 Info: Blue (#3B82F6), 24-hour response, no escalation
- P4 Success: Green (#22C55E), no response required

**2. Alert Categories:**
- Maintenance: PM overdue/critical/approaching, inspections, fault codes
- Mileage (RFQ Section 5): Overrun/underrun alerts, fleet imbalance
- Assignment: No suitable train, conflicts, schedule not published
- Resource: Man-hour shortage, track overbooked
- Cost: Budget exceeded/warning, variance
- System: Integration failures, sync delays

**3. Notification Types:**
- Toast: Transient, top-right, 5s auto-dismiss, max 3 stacked
- Inline: Contextual page alerts, dismissible
- Actionable: Require user decision, up to 3 CTAs
- Modal: Blocking critical decisions

**4. Notification Center:**
- Bell icon with badge count (pulse for critical)
- Tabs: All, Unread, Critical, Actions
- Grouped by date (Today, Yesterday, etc.)
- Quick actions: Schedule PM, View Details, Acknowledge

**5. Delivery Channels:**
- In-App: All alert types
- Push: P1, P2, most P3 (configurable)
- Email: All types + daily digest option
- SMS: P1 critical only (optional)

**6. Escalation Workflow (4 tiers):**
- Tier 1: Assigned user, 15 min wait
- Tier 2: Supervisor, 30 min wait
- Tier 3: Management, 1 hour wait
- Tier 4: Executive, phone call option

**7. User Preferences:**
- Channel toggles (In-App/Push/Email/SMS)
- Quiet hours with critical override
- Daily digest with configurable time
- Category-specific channel preferences
- Frequency limits with bundling

**8. Snooze Options:**
- 15 minutes, 1 hour, 4 hours
- Tomorrow morning (9 AM)
- Custom duration
- P1 alerts cannot be snoozed

### TypeScript Types Created:
- AlertPriority, AlertPriorityConfig, AlertCategory, AlertCategoryConfig
- 6 alert type unions (Maintenance, Mileage, Assignment, Resource, Cost, System)
- Notification, NotificationListItem, NotificationAction
- DeliveryChannel, DeliveryStatus, DeliveryRecord, ChannelConfig
- EscalationTier, EscalationTierConfig, EscalationHistoryEntry
- QuietHoursConfig, DailyDigestConfig, FrequencyLimitConfig
- NotificationPreferences, AlertSubscription, SnoozeRequest
- NotificationCenterState, PreferencesEditorState, Toast, ToastContainerState
- AlertAnalytics, NotificationWebhookPayload
- Helper functions: formatTimeAgo, canAcknowledge/Snooze/Resolve, calculateSnoozeUntil

### Unknown Unknowns Documented (20):
1. Alert fatigue threshold determination
2. Cross-shift handoff handling
3. Timezone handling for distributed teams
4. Alert correlation and grouping
5. False positive rate and threshold tuning
6. Network reliability for push notifications
7. Audit retention requirements
8. GDPR compliance for user data in notifications
9. Multi-language support
10. Role change impact on subscriptions
...and 10 more in docs

### Gestalt Principles Applied:
- Proximity: Related actions grouped
- Similarity: Consistent color coding
- Enclosure: Card boundaries for notifications
- Continuity: Timeline ordering
- Figure-Ground: Elevated toasts, modal overlays
- Common Fate: Batch selection animations

## MILEAGE-UX-010 [COMPLETE] - Mobile Responsive Design

### Created Files:
1. docs/ux/mobile-responsive-design.md - Full 17-section UX specification
2. src/mileage/types/mobile-ui.ts - Comprehensive TypeScript types

### Research Sources:
- Hicron: Fleet Management Dashboard Design
- Volpis: UX Design of Fleet Management Apps
- Standard Beagle: Fleet Tablet App Design
- Medium: Railway Maintenance SaaS UX Design (field worker considerations)
- Google: PWA Offline-First Architecture
- MDN: Offline and Background Operation

### Key Design Decisions:

**1. Responsive Breakpoints:**
- xs (<576px): Mobile, single column
- sm (576-767px): Mobile large, single column
- md (768-991px): Tablet portrait, 2 columns
- lg (992-1199px): Tablet landscape, 3 columns
- xl (>=1200px): Desktop, full layout

**2. Navigation Patterns:**
- Desktop: Horizontal top navigation bar
- Tablet: Condensed tabs + hamburger for secondary
- Mobile: Bottom tab bar (5 tabs max) + slide-out menu

**3. Card-Based Layout (Mobile):**
- Replace data tables with cards on mobile
- Collapsible sections for complex data
- Horizontal scroll for essential tables

**4. Touch Interactions:**
- Minimum 48x48px touch targets
- Swipe gestures (left/right for actions)
- Pull-to-refresh, pinch-to-zoom

**5. Offline-First Design:**
- App shell architecture (cached shell, dynamic content)
- Service worker caching strategies
- Optimistic UI updates with background sync
- Offline indicators and sync status

**6. Field Worker Optimizations:**
- High contrast mode for outdoor glare
- Large touch targets (64px) for gloves
- Voice input support
- QR code scanning for train check-in

**7. Role-Based Mobile Views:**
- Operations: Schedule-focused, assignment management
- Maintenance: Task list, check-in flow, inspections
- Management: KPIs, approvals, escalations

**8. PWA Features:**
- Web app manifest for installation
- Service worker for offline support
- Background sync for queued actions
- Push notifications

### TypeScript Types Created:
- Breakpoint, BreakpointConfig, DeviceType
- NavigationItem, BottomTabItem, MobileNavigationState
- MobileTrainCard, MobileTaskCard, MobileKPICard
- BottomSheetConfig, BottomSheetState
- MobileFilterOption, MobileFilterGroup, MobileFilterState
- SwipeDirection, SwipeActionConfig, ListItemSwipeActions
- NetworkStatus, SyncStatus, OfflineQueueItem, OfflineState
- PWAInstallState, PWAUpdateState, ServiceWorkerState
- TouchTargetSpec, MobileRole, AccessibilityPreferences
- PerformanceMetrics, PerformanceBudget, MobileAppState
- Helper functions: getCurrentBreakpoint, isMobile/Tablet/Desktop, formatMileageMobile

### Unknown Unknowns Documented (20):
1. Data usage concerns for field workers
2. Battery consumption from background sync
3. Android device fragmentation
4. Network reliability in rural/underground areas
5. iOS PWA limitations
6. Biometric auth device differences
...and 14 more in docs

### Gestalt Principles Applied:
- Proximity: Related actions grouped in bottom sheets
- Similarity: Consistent card styling, color coding
- Enclosure: Card boundaries, section separators
- Continuity: Smooth scroll, gesture continuity
- Figure-Ground: Elevated bottom sheets, modal overlays
- Common Fate: List items scroll together

## CLAUDE-CODE-001: Replace Claude API with Claude Code CLI

### Problem Statement:
- Current implementation uses @anthropic-ai/sdk requiring ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
- User wants to use Claude Code CLI (`claude` command) instead
- Claude Code is already authenticated locally, no API key needed

### Research Completed:
Claude Code CLI supports:
- `-p, --print` for non-interactive mode (useful for pipes)
- `--output-format json|stream-json` for structured output
- `--system-prompt <prompt>` for system prompts
- `--model <model>` for model selection (opus, sonnet)
- `--input-format text|stream-json` for input

Command format:
```bash
claude -p --output-format json --system-prompt "system" "prompt"
```

### Implementation Plan:
1. Create src/clients/claude-code.ts - subprocess-based client
2. Maintain same interface as claude.ts for compatibility
3. Update harness/index.ts to use new client
4. Test E2E with real Claude Code
5. Update documentation

### Files to Modify:
- src/clients/claude-code.ts (NEW)
- src/harness/index.ts (update import)
- src/index.ts (update export)
- CLAUDE.md (update documentation)

1вҶ’# Centralised Dashboard - UX Specification
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’**Document ID:** MILEAGE-UX-003
     4вҶ’**Version:** 1.0
     5вҶ’**Created:** 2026-01-08
     6вҶ’**Status:** Design Specification
     7вҶ’**RFQ Reference:** Section 5 - Software Output
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’---
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’## 1. Executive Summary
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’The Centralised Dashboard is the **primary entry point** for all users of the Mileage Management System. Per RFQ Section 5, it must provide:
    14вҶ’- One **high-level customised output** (executive summary view)
    15вҶ’- **Complete data and mileage status** (detailed operational view)
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’This document defines the UX architecture, wireframes, components, and interactions following Gestalt principles and industry best practices.
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’---
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’## 2. RFQ Requirements Mapping
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’| RFQ Requirement | Dashboard Component | Priority |
    24вҶ’|-----------------|---------------------|----------|
    25вҶ’| Centralised dashboard with high-level customised output | Hero KPI Cards + Executive Summary | P0 |
    26вҶ’| Complete data, mileage status | Fleet Status Grid + Detailed Tables | P0 |
    27вҶ’| Current maintenance status of all trains | Maintenance Status Panel | P0 |
    28вҶ’| Train run assignments | Run Assignment Widget | P0 |
    29вҶ’| Completed and upcoming PM/overhaul activities | PM Schedule Timeline | P0 |
    30вҶ’| Maintenance compliance status | Compliance Gauge + Alerts | P0 |
    31вҶ’| Monthly and yearly cost compliance | Cost Comparison Chart | P1 |
    32вҶ’| Simulation report access | Quick Action Links | P1 |
    33вҶ’| Email notification status | Notification Center | P2 |
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’---
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’## 3. User Roles & Views
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’### 3.1 Role-Based Dashboard Customisation
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’| Role | Primary Focus | Default Widgets |
    42вҶ’|------|---------------|-----------------|
    43вҶ’| **Fleet Manager** | Overall fleet health, compliance | KPI Cards, Fleet Grid, Compliance |
    44вҶ’| **Maintenance Planner** | PM scheduling, resource allocation | PM Timeline, Resource Chart, Warnings |
    45вҶ’| **Operations Manager** | Daily run assignments, fitness | Run Assignments, Fitness Status, Alerts |
    46вҶ’| **Executive** | Cost compliance, high-level metrics | Executive Summary, Cost Charts, Trends |
    47вҶ’| **Technician Supervisor** | Work orders, track allocation | Today's Work, Track Status, Backlog |
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’### 3.2 View Modes
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’1. **Executive View** (Default for Executives)
    52вҶ’   - Single-page summary
    53вҶ’   - Large KPI cards
    54вҶ’   - Trend sparklines
    55вҶ’   - No drill-down required
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’2. **Operational View** (Default for Planners/Managers)
    58вҶ’   - Multi-panel layout
    59вҶ’   - Interactive charts
    60вҶ’   - Filterable tables
    61вҶ’   - Drill-down capability
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’3. **Focus View** (Customisable)
    64вҶ’   - User-selected widgets
    65вҶ’   - Saved layouts per user
    66вҶ’   - Quick switching
    67вҶ’

   287вҶ’## 6. Gestalt Principles Application
   288вҶ’
   289вҶ’### 6.1 Proximity
   290вҶ’- **KPI Cards**: Grouped horizontally, equal spacing (24px gap)
   291вҶ’- **Fleet Panels**: Kinki Sharyo and Alstom adjacent, separated by 16px
   292вҶ’- **Related metrics**: Grouped within same card boundary
   293вҶ’
   294вҶ’### 6.2 Similarity
   295вҶ’- **Consistent card styling**: All panels use same border-radius (12px), shadow, padding
   296вҶ’- **Status colours**: Same palette across all components
   297вҶ’- **Typography**: Consistent font sizes per hierarchy level
   298вҶ’
   299вҶ’### 6.3 Enclosure
   300вҶ’- **Card boundaries**: Clear container borders for each widget
   301вҶ’- **Section headers**: Visual separation between dashboard sections
   302вҶ’- **Modal dialogs**: Distinct overlay with backdrop blur
   303вҶ’
   304вҶ’### 6.4 Continuity
   305вҶ’- **Timeline**: Continuous horizontal flow, left-to-right
   306вҶ’- **Reading pattern**: Z-pattern layout (top-left вҶ’ top-right вҶ’ bottom-left вҶ’ bottom-right)
   307вҶ’- **Data flow**: KPIs вҶ’ Status вҶ’ Actions вҶ’ Details
   308вҶ’
   309вҶ’### 6.5 Closure
   310вҶ’- **Progress bars**: User mentally completes the "fill" to understand percentage
   311вҶ’- **Trend indicators**: Arrow direction implies continuation of trend
   312вҶ’- **Abbreviated numbers**: "3.2K" understood as full number
   313вҶ’
   314вҶ’### 6.6 Figure-Ground
   315вҶ’- **Light background**: #F9FAFB (gray-50)
   316вҶ’- **Card background**: #FFFFFF with subtle shadow
   317вҶ’- **Focus elements**: Higher contrast, darker text
   318вҶ’

   354вҶ’
   355вҶ’### 8.1 Desktop (вүҘ1440px)

   378вҶ’## 9. Interaction Patterns
   379вҶ’
   380вҶ’### 9.1 KPI Card Interactions
   381вҶ’- **Hover**: Subtle elevation increase, show trend tooltip
   382вҶ’- **Click**: Navigate to detailed view (e.g., Fleet Availability вҶ’ Fleet Grid)
   383вҶ’- **Long press (mobile)**: Show quick actions menu
   384вҶ’
   385вҶ’### 9.2 Alert Interactions
   386вҶ’- **Dismiss**: Removes from view (can be restored from notification center)
   387вҶ’- **Action button**: Navigate to relevant section with context
   388вҶ’- **Expand**: Show full alert details inline
   389вҶ’
   390вҶ’### 9.3 Timeline Interactions
   391вҶ’- **Hover on marker**: Show PM details tooltip
   392вҶ’- **Click marker**: Open PM detail modal
   393вҶ’- **Drag**: Pan timeline left/right
   394вҶ’- **Pinch (touch)**: Zoom in/out
   395вҶ’
   396вҶ’### 9.4 Table Interactions
   397вҶ’- **Row click**: Navigate to train/assignment detail
   398вҶ’- **Column header click**: Sort ascending/descending
   399вҶ’- **Filter icon**: Open filter dropdown
   400вҶ’- **Checkbox**: Bulk selection for actions
   401вҶ’

   416вҶ’```

   418вҶ’### 10.3 Contextual Actions (Per Widget)
   419вҶ’
   420вҶ’- **Refresh**: Reload widget data
   421вҶ’- **Expand**: Full-screen view
   422вҶ’- **Export**: Download as PNG/CSV
   423вҶ’- **Configure**: Widget settings
   424вҶ’- **Help**: Contextual documentation
   425вҶ’
   426вҶ’---
   427вҶ’
   428вҶ’## 11. Data Refresh Strategy
   429вҶ’
   430вҶ’| Data Type | Refresh Interval | Source |
   431вҶ’|-----------|------------------|--------|
   432вҶ’| Fleet Status | Real-time (WebSocket) | Maximo Integration |
   433вҶ’| Mileage Data | Every 5 minutes | Daily Feed + Live Updates |
   434вҶ’| PM Schedule | Every 15 minutes | Scheduler Service |
   435вҶ’| Resource Data | Hourly | Resource Leveler Service |
   436вҶ’| Cost Data | Daily (overnight) | Cost Calculator Service |
   437вҶ’| Alerts | Real-time (Push) | Alert Service |
   438вҶ’
   439вҶ’**Visual Indicator:**
   440вҶ’- Last updated timestamp on each widget
   441вҶ’- Subtle pulse animation during refresh
   442вҶ’- Stale data warning (>30 min old)
   443вҶ’
   444вҶ’---
   445вҶ’
   446вҶ’## 12. Accessibility Requirements
   447вҶ’
   448вҶ’### 12.1 WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance
   449вҶ’- **Colour contrast**: Minimum 4.5:1 for text
   450вҶ’- **Focus indicators**: Visible focus ring on all interactive elements
   451вҶ’- **Keyboard navigation**: Full keyboard support with logical tab order
   452вҶ’- **Screen reader**: ARIA labels on all interactive elements
   453вҶ’
   454вҶ’### 12.2 Colour-Blind Considerations
   455вҶ’- Use icons + colour for status indication
   456вҶ’- Pattern fills in charts as alternative to colour only
   457вҶ’- High contrast mode option
   458вҶ’
   459вҶ’### 12.3 Motion Preferences
   460вҶ’- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` media query
   461вҶ’- Disable auto-playing animations when set
   462вҶ’- Provide static alternatives to animated content
   463вҶ’
   464вҶ’---
   465вҶ’
   466вҶ’## 13. Unknown Unknowns Discovered
   467вҶ’
   468вҶ’### 13.1 Technical Unknowns
   469вҶ’1. **WebSocket reliability**: What happens when connection drops? Need reconnection strategy and offline indicators
   470вҶ’2. **Data staleness**: How to handle cases where Maximo feed is delayed >1 hour?
   471вҶ’3. **Cross-timezone operations**: Are all users in same timezone? How to display times for distributed teams?
   472вҶ’
   473вҶ’### 13.2 User Behaviour Unknowns
   474вҶ’4. **Widget customisation adoption**: Will users actually customise, or stick with defaults?
   475вҶ’5. **Alert fatigue**: How many alerts before users start ignoring them? Need smart grouping
   476вҶ’6. **Mobile usage patterns**: When/where do users access on mobile? Different feature set needed?
   477вҶ’
   478вҶ’### 13.3 Business Logic Unknowns
   479вҶ’7. **PM rescheduling authority**: Who can defer PM? What approval workflow needed?
   480вҶ’8. **Cost variance tolerance**: Is Вұ5% acceptable for all categories, or category-specific?
   481вҶ’9. **Run assignment conflicts**: What happens when Operations and Maintenance recommend different assignments?
   482вҶ’
   483вҶ’### 13.4 Integration Unknowns
   484вҶ’10. **Maximo sync latency**: What's typical delay between Maximo update and dashboard reflection?
   485вҶ’11. **Email notification reliability**: What if emails bounce? Need in-app fallback?
   486вҶ’12. **Report generation time**: Large reports (12-year projection) - sync or async?
   487вҶ’
   488вҶ’---
   489вҶ’
   490вҶ’## 14. Competitor Inspiration
   491вҶ’
   492вҶ’### 14.1 IBM Maximo
   493вҶ’- Role-based preset dashboards
   494вҶ’- KPI cards with drill-down
   495вҶ’- Work order list with status colours
   496вҶ’
   497вҶ’### 14.2 Siemens Rail
   498вҶ’- Geospatial map integration
   499вҶ’- Real-time performance indicators
   500вҶ’- Predictive analytics visualisation
   501вҶ’
   502вҶ’### 14.3 voestalpine zentrak
   503вҶ’- 360В° asset view
   504вҶ’- User-customisable KPI dashboard
   505вҶ’- Standard + custom KPI filters
   506вҶ’
   507вҶ’### 14.4 Modern SaaS Dashboards (Dribbble/Behance)
   508вҶ’- Dark mode support
   509вҶ’- Glassmorphism cards
   510вҶ’- Micro-interactions on hover
   511вҶ’- Data density with clarity
   512вҶ’
   513вҶ’---
   514вҶ’
   515вҶ’## 15. Implementation Notes
   516вҶ’
   517вҶ’### 15.1 Technology Recommendations
   518вҶ’- **Frontend**: React + TypeScript
   519вҶ’- **Charts**: Apache ECharts or Recharts
   520вҶ’- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state
   521вҶ’- **Design System**: Tailwind CSS + Headless UI
   522вҶ’- **Real-time**: Socket.io for WebSocket
   523вҶ’
   524вҶ’### 15.2 Performance Targets
   525вҶ’- **First Contentful Paint**: < 1.5s
   526вҶ’- **Time to Interactive**: < 3s
   527вҶ’- **Largest Contentful Paint**: < 2.5s
   528вҶ’- **Cumulative Layout Shift**: < 0.1
   529вҶ’
   530вҶ’### 15.3 Testing Requirements
   531вҶ’- Visual regression testing with Playwright
   532вҶ’- Accessibility audit with axe-core
   533вҶ’- Performance monitoring with Lighthouse CI
   534вҶ’- E2E tests for all user flows
   535вҶ’
   536вҶ’---
   537вҶ’
   538вҶ’## 16. Sources & References
   539вҶ’
   540вҶ’### Fleet Management UX
   541вҶ’- [Hicron: Fleet Management Dashboard Design Guide](https://hicronsoftware.com/blog/fleet-management-dashboard-design/)
   542вҶ’- [Smashing Magazine: UX Strategies for Real-Time Dashboards](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/09/ux-strategies-real-time-dashboards/)
   543вҶ’- [Aufait UX: Dashboard Design for Logistics](https://www.aufaitux.com/blog/dashboard-design-logistics-supply-chain-ux/)
   544вҶ’
   545вҶ’### Railway & Maintenance Systems
   546вҶ’- [ESRI: Railway Operations Management](https://www.esri.com/en-us/industries/rail/business-areas/operations)
   547вҶ’- [voestalpine: Asset & Maintenance Management](https://www.voestalpine.com/railway-systems/en/products/asset-and-maintenance-management/)
   548вҶ’- [Siemens: System Performance Dashboard](https://www.mobility.siemens.com/global/en/portfolio/rail-infrastructure/mass-transit/railway-mass-transit-analysis.html)
   549вҶ’
   550вҶ’### Design Principles
   551вҶ’- [Playfair Data: Applying Gestalt Principles to Dashboard Design](https://playfairdata.com/applying-gestalt-principles-to-dashboard-design/)
   552вҶ’- [BricxLabs: 15 Dashboard Design Principles for 2025](https://bricxlabs.com/blogs/tips-for-dashboard-design)
   553вҶ’- [Dev3lop: Gestalt Principles in Analytics Dashboard Design](https://dev3lop.com/gestalt-principles-applied-to-analytics-dashboard-design/)
   554вҶ’
   555вҶ’### Visual Inspiration
   556вҶ’- [Dribbble: Dark Mode Dashboard Designs](https://dribbble.com/tags/dark-mode-dashboard)
   557вҶ’- [Behance: Dashboard Analytics Projects](https://www.behance.net/search/projects/dashboard%20analytics)
   558вҶ’- [Muzli: 60+ Dashboard Design Inspiration](https://muz.li/inspiration/dashboard-inspiration/)
   559вҶ’
   560вҶ’### IBM Maximo
   561вҶ’- [IBM: Maximo Application Suite](https://www.ibm.com/products/maximo)
   562вҶ’- [IBM: Maximo for Transportation](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/mft/7.6.2?topic=product-overview)
   563вҶ’
   564вҶ’---
   565вҶ’
   566вҶ’## 17. Appendix: Wireframe Grid System
   567вҶ’
   568вҶ’### 17.1 12-Column Grid

# Centralised Dashboard - UX Specification

**Document ID:** MILEAGE-UX-003
**Version:** 1.0
**Created:** 2026-01-08
**Status:** Design Specification
**RFQ Reference:** Section 5 - Software Output

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Centralised Dashboard is the **primary entry point** for all users of the Mileage Management System. Per RFQ Section 5, it must provide:
- One **high-level customised output** (executive summary view)
- **Complete data and mileage status** (detailed operational view)

This document defines the UX architecture, wireframes, components, and interactions following Gestalt principles and industry best practices.

---

## 2. RFQ Requirements Mapping

| RFQ Requirement | Dashboard Component | Priority |
|-----------------|---------------------|----------|
| Centralised dashboard with high-level customised output | Hero KPI Cards + Executive Summary | P0 |
| Complete data, mileage status | Fleet Status Grid + Detailed Tables | P0 |
| Current maintenance status of all trains | Maintenance Status Panel | P0 |
| Train run assignments | Run Assignment Widget | P0 |
| Completed and upcoming PM/overhaul activities | PM Schedule Timeline | P0 |
| Maintenance compliance status | Compliance Gauge + Alerts | P0 |
| Monthly and yearly cost compliance | Cost Comparison Chart | P1 |
| Simulation report access | Quick Action Links | P1 |
| Email notification status | Notification Center | P2 |

---

## 3. User Roles & Views

### 3.1 Role-Based Dashboard Customisation

| Role | Primary Focus | Default Widgets |
|------|---------------|-----------------|
| **Fleet Manager** | Overall fleet health, compliance | KPI Cards, Fleet Grid, Compliance |
| **Maintenance Planner** | PM scheduling, resource allocation | PM Timeline, Resource Chart, Warnings |
| **Operations Manager** | Daily run assignments, fitness | Run Assignments, Fitness Status, Alerts |
| **Executive** | Cost compliance, high-level metrics | Executive Summary, Cost Charts, Trends |
| **Technician Supervisor** | Work orders, track allocation | Today's Work, Track Status, Backlog |

### 3.2 View Modes

1. **Executive View** (Default for Executives)
   - Single-page summary
   - Large KPI cards
   - Trend sparklines
   - No drill-down required

2. **Operational View** (Default for Planners/Managers)
   - Multi-panel layout
   - Interactive charts
   - Filterable tables
   - Drill-down capability

3. **Focus View** (Customisable)
   - User-selected widgets
   - Saved layouts per user
   - Quick switching

---

## 4. Information Architecture

### 4.1 Dashboard Hierarchy (Gestalt: Proximity + Enclosure)

### 4.2 Visual Hierarchy (Gestalt: Similarity + Continuity)

**Level 1 - Primary Focus (Top)**
- KPI Cards: Largest text, high contrast, immediate visibility
- Critical alerts: Red/amber visual weight

**Level 2 - Secondary Context (Middle)**
- Fleet status panels: Medium-weight cards
- PM timeline: Linear flow, left-to-right reading

**Level 3 - Supporting Detail (Bottom)**
- Charts: Data-dense but scannable
- Tables: Detailed information on demand

---

**KPI Card Configuration:**

| KPI | Formula | Warning Threshold | Critical Threshold |
|-----|---------|-------------------|-------------------|
| Fleet Availability | (FIT trains / Total) Г— 100 | < 90% | < 85% |
| PM Compliance | (On-time PM / Due PM) Г— 100 | < 95% | < 90% |
| Resource Utilisation | (Used man-hours / Available) Г— 100 | > 95% or < 70% | > 100% or < 60% |
| Cost Variance | ((Actual - Budget) / Budget) Г— 100 | > Вұ5% | > Вұ10% |

### 5.4 PM Schedule Timeline

**Timeline Configuration:**
- Default view: 30 days
- Zoom levels: 7d, 14d, 30d, 90d, 1Y
- Colour coding by PM level
- Hover reveals: Train ID, PM type, scheduled date, status

**PM Level Colours:**
| Level | Colour | Hex |
|-------|--------|-----|
| PM1 | Blue | #3B82F6 |
| PM2 | Indigo | #6366F1 |
| PM3 | Purple | #8B5CF6 |
| OVERHAUL | Orange | #F97316 |

**Status Markers:**
- в—Ҹ Filled circle: Completed
- в—Ӣ Empty circle: Scheduled
- в—җ Half-filled: In Progress
- вҠ— Crossed: Overdue

### 5.5 Resource Utilisation Chart

**Chart Type:** Stacked Area with Line Overlay (as designed in MILEAGE-UX-002)

**Data Series:**
1. **Available Man-hours** (Area, light blue fill)
2. **Adjusted Workload** (Area, green fill when under, red when over)
3. **Predicted Maintenance Workload** (Line, dashed orange)

**Gap Severity Colours:**
| Gap | Colour | Meaning |
|-----|--------|---------|
| > 500 | #22C55E Green | Comfortable capacity |
| 0 to 500 | #EAB308 Yellow | Near capacity |
| -500 to 0 | #F97316 Orange | Slight overallocation |
| < -500 | #EF4444 Red | Critical overallocation |

```

---

## 6. Gestalt Principles Application

### 6.1 Proximity
- **KPI Cards**: Grouped horizontally, equal spacing (24px gap)
- **Fleet Panels**: Kinki Sharyo and Alstom adjacent, separated by 16px
- **Related metrics**: Grouped within same card boundary

### 6.2 Similarity
- **Consistent card styling**: All panels use same border-radius (12px), shadow, padding
- **Status colours**: Same palette across all components
- **Typography**: Consistent font sizes per hierarchy level

### 6.3 Enclosure
- **Card boundaries**: Clear container borders for each widget
- **Section headers**: Visual separation between dashboard sections
- **Modal dialogs**: Distinct overlay with backdrop blur

### 6.4 Continuity
- **Timeline**: Continuous horizontal flow, left-to-right
- **Reading pattern**: Z-pattern layout (top-left вҶ’ top-right вҶ’ bottom-left вҶ’ bottom-right)
- **Data flow**: KPIs вҶ’ Status вҶ’ Actions вҶ’ Details

### 6.5 Closure
- **Progress bars**: User mentally completes the "fill" to understand percentage
- **Trend indicators**: Arrow direction implies continuation of trend
- **Abbreviated numbers**: "3.2K" understood as full number

### 6.6 Figure-Ground
- **Light background**: #F9FAFB (gray-50)
- **Card background**: #FFFFFF with subtle shadow
- **Focus elements**: Higher contrast, darker text

---

## 7. Colour System

### 7.1 Primary Palette

| Usage | Light Mode | Dark Mode |
|-------|------------|-----------|
| Background | #F9FAFB | #111827 |
| Card Background | #FFFFFF | #1F2937 |
| Primary Text | #111827 | #F9FAFB |
| Secondary Text | #6B7280 | #9CA3AF |
| Border | #E5E7EB | #374151 |
| Primary Action | #3B82F6 | #60A5FA |

### 7.2 Status Colours

| Status | Background | Text/Icon | Border |
|--------|------------|-----------|--------|
| Success | #F0FDF4 | #166534 | #22C55E |
| Warning | #FFFBEB | #854D0E | #F59E0B |
| Error | #FEF2F2 | #991B1B | #EF4444 |
| Info | #EFF6FF | #1E40AF | #3B82F6 |
| Neutral | #F9FAFB | #374151 | #D1D5DB |

### 7.3 Fleet Colours

| Fleet | Primary | Light | Dark |
|-------|---------|-------|------|
| Kinki Sharyo | #0891B2 | #CFFAFE | #164E63 |
| Alstom | #7C3AED | #EDE9FE | #4C1D95 |

---

## 8. Responsive Breakpoints

### 8.1 Desktop (вүҘ1440px)
- Full 4-column KPI layout
- Side-by-side panels
- All widgets visible

### 8.2 Laptop (1024px - 1439px)
- 4-column KPI (compressed)
- 2-column panel layout
- Horizontal scroll on tables

### 8.3 Tablet (768px - 1023px)
- 2-column KPI layout
- Stacked panels
- Collapsible sections

### 8.4 Mobile (< 768px)
- Single column
- Card-based layout
- Bottom navigation
- Swipe for more KPIs

---

## 9. Interaction Patterns

### 9.1 KPI Card Interactions
- **Hover**: Subtle elevation increase, show trend tooltip
- **Click**: Navigate to detailed view (e.g., Fleet Availability вҶ’ Fleet Grid)
- **Long press (mobile)**: Show quick actions menu

### 9.2 Alert Interactions
- **Dismiss**: Removes from view (can be restored from notification center)
- **Action button**: Navigate to relevant section with context
- **Expand**: Show full alert details inline

### 9.3 Timeline Interactions
- **Hover on marker**: Show PM details tooltip
- **Click marker**: Open PM detail modal
- **Drag**: Pan timeline left/right
- **Pinch (touch)**: Zoom in/out

### 9.4 Table Interactions
- **Row click**: Navigate to train/assignment detail
- **Column header click**: Sort ascending/descending
- **Filter icon**: Open filter dropdown
- **Checkbox**: Bulk selection for actions

---

## 10. Navigation Structure

### 10.1 Primary Navigation (Top Bar)

```
[Logo] | Dashboard | Planning | Assignments | Reports | Simulations | [Search] | [Notifications] | [User]
```

### 10.2 Dashboard Sub-navigation (Tabs)

```
[Overview] [Fleet Status] [Maintenance] [Resources] [Cost]
```

### 10.3 Contextual Actions (Per Widget)

- **Refresh**: Reload widget data
- **Expand**: Full-screen view
- **Export**: Download as PNG/CSV
- **Configure**: Widget settings
- **Help**: Contextual documentation

---

## 11. Data Refresh Strategy

| Data Type | Refresh Interval | Source |
|-----------|------------------|--------|
| Fleet Status | Real-time (WebSocket) | Maximo Integration |
| Mileage Data | Every 5 minutes | Daily Feed + Live Updates |
| PM Schedule | Every 15 minutes | Scheduler Service |
| Resource Data | Hourly | Resource Leveler Service |
| Cost Data | Daily (overnight) | Cost Calculator Service |
| Alerts | Real-time (Push) | Alert Service |

**Visual Indicator:**
- Last updated timestamp on each widget
- Subtle pulse animation during refresh
- Stale data warning (>30 min old)

---

## 12. Accessibility Requirements

### 12.1 WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance
- **Colour contrast**: Minimum 4.5:1 for text
- **Focus indicators**: Visible focus ring on all interactive elements
- **Keyboard navigation**: Full keyboard support with logical tab order
- **Screen reader**: ARIA labels on all interactive elements

### 12.2 Colour-Blind Considerations
- Use icons + colour for status indication
- Pattern fills in charts as alternative to colour only
- High contrast mode option

### 12.3 Motion Preferences
- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` media query
- Disable auto-playing animations when set
- Provide static alternatives to animated content

---

## 13. Unknown Unknowns Discovered

### 13.1 Technical Unknowns
1. **WebSocket reliability**: What happens when connection drops? Need reconnection strategy and offline indicators
2. **Data staleness**: How to handle cases where Maximo feed is delayed >1 hour?
3. **Cross-timezone operations**: Are all users in same timezone? How to display times for distributed teams?

### 13.2 User Behaviour Unknowns
4. **Widget customisation adoption**: Will users actually customise, or stick with defaults?
5. **Alert fatigue**: How many alerts before users start ignoring them? Need smart grouping
6. **Mobile usage patterns**: When/where do users access on mobile? Different feature set needed?

### 13.3 Business Logic Unknowns
7. **PM rescheduling authority**: Who can defer PM? What approval workflow needed?
8. **Cost variance tolerance**: Is Вұ5% acceptable for all categories, or category-specific?
9. **Run assignment conflicts**: What happens when Operations and Maintenance recommend different assignments?

### 13.4 Integration Unknowns
10. **Maximo sync latency**: What's typical delay between Maximo update and dashboard reflection?
11. **Email notification reliability**: What if emails bounce? Need in-app fallback?
12. **Report generation time**: Large reports (12-year projection) - sync or async?

---

## 14. Competitor Inspiration

### 14.1 IBM Maximo
- Role-based preset dashboards
- KPI cards with drill-down
- Work order list with status colours

### 14.2 Siemens Rail
- Geospatial map integration
- Real-time performance indicators
- Predictive analytics visualisation

### 14.3 voestalpine zentrak
- 360В° asset view
- User-customisable KPI dashboard
- Standard + custom KPI filters

### 14.4 Modern SaaS Dashboards (Dribbble/Behance)
- Dark mode support
- Glassmorphism cards
- Micro-interactions on hover
- Data density with clarity

---

## 15. Implementation Notes

### 15.1 Technology Recommendations
- **Frontend**: React + TypeScript
- **Charts**: Apache ECharts or Recharts
- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state
- **Design System**: Tailwind CSS + Headless UI
- **Real-time**: Socket.io for WebSocket

### 15.2 Performance Targets
- **First Contentful Paint**: < 1.5s
- **Time to Interactive**: < 3s
- **Largest Contentful Paint**: < 2.5s
- **Cumulative Layout Shift**: < 0.1

### 15.3 Testing Requirements
- Visual regression testing with Playwright
- Accessibility audit with axe-core
- Performance monitoring with Lighthouse CI
- E2E tests for all user flows

---

## 16. Sources & References

### Fleet Management UX
- [Hicron: Fleet Management Dashboard Design Guide](https://hicronsoftware.com/blog/fleet-management-dashboard-design/)
- [Smashing Magazine: UX Strategies for Real-Time Dashboards](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/09/ux-strategies-real-time-dashboards/)
- [Aufait UX: Dashboard Design for Logistics](https://www.aufaitux.com/blog/dashboard-design-logistics-supply-chain-ux/)

### Railway & Maintenance Systems
- [ESRI: Railway Operations Management](https://www.esri.com/en-us/industries/rail/business-areas/operations)
- [voestalpine: Asset & Maintenance Management](https://www.voestalpine.com/railway-systems/en/products/asset-and-maintenance-management/)
- [Siemens: System Performance Dashboard](https://www.mobility.siemens.com/global/en/portfolio/rail-infrastructure/mass-transit/railway-mass-transit-analysis.html)

### Design Principles
- [Playfair Data: Applying Gestalt Principles to Dashboard Design](https://playfairdata.com/applying-gestalt-principles-to-dashboard-design/)
- [BricxLabs: 15 Dashboard Design Principles for 2025](https://bricxlabs.com/blogs/tips-for-dashboard-design)
- [Dev3lop: Gestalt Principles in Analytics Dashboard Design](https://dev3lop.com/gestalt-principles-applied-to-analytics-dashboard-design/)

### Visual Inspiration
- [Dribbble: Dark Mode Dashboard Designs](https://dribbble.com/tags/dark-mode-dashboard)
- [Behance: Dashboard Analytics Projects](https://www.behance.net/search/projects/dashboard%20analytics)
- [Muzli: 60+ Dashboard Design Inspiration](https://muz.li/inspiration/dashboard-inspiration/)

### IBM Maximo
- [IBM: Maximo Application Suite](https://www.ibm.com/products/maximo)
- [IBM: Maximo for Transportation](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/mft/7.6.2?topic=product-overview)

---

## 17. Appendix: Wireframe Grid System

### 17.1 12-Column Grid

```
Desktop (1440px):
в”Ӯ 48px в”Ӯ Col в”Ӯ Gap в”Ӯ Col в”Ӯ Gap в”Ӯ Col в”Ӯ Gap в”Ӯ Col в”Ӯ Gap в”Ӯ Col в”Ӯ Gap в”Ӯ Col в”Ӯ Gap в”Ӯ Col в”Ӯ Gap в”Ӯ Col в”Ӯ Gap в”Ӯ Col в”Ӯ Gap в”Ӯ Col в”Ӯ Gap в”Ӯ Col в”Ӯ Gap в”Ӯ Col в”Ӯ 48px в”Ӯ

### 17.2 Widget Sizing

| Widget | Desktop Cols | Tablet Cols | Mobile Cols |
|--------|--------------|-------------|-------------|
| KPI Card | 3 | 6 | 12 |
| Fleet Panel | 6 | 12 | 12 |

1вҶ’# Man-Hour Projection Chart - UX Design Specification
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’**Task ID:** MILEAGE-UX-002
     4вҶ’**Status:** CRITICAL - RFQ Section 8 Case Study
     5вҶ’**Created:** 2026-01-08
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’---
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’## 1. Executive Summary
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’The Man-Hour Projection Chart is the **core visualization** for resource planning in the Rolling Stock Mileage Management System. It displays workload (man-hours required) vs capacity (man-hours available) over a 12-year contract period, enabling planners to:
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’1. Identify resource overallocation/underallocation
    14вҶ’2. Compare "Without adjustment" vs "With adjustment" scenarios
    15вҶ’3. Make data-driven decisions about maintenance scheduling
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’**RFQ Reference:** Section 8 - Case Studies (Page 4)
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’---
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’## 2. Chart Type Selection
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’### Primary Chart: Stacked Area/Line Combination Chart
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’Based on RFQ Section 8 and [industry best practices](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/what-is-a-resource-histogram):
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’| Chart Element | Type | Purpose |
    28вҶ’|---------------|------|---------|
    29вҶ’| Availability (Capacity) | Solid Area | Shows available man-hours as baseline |
    30вҶ’| Adjusted Workload | Line with markers | Shows scheduled/leveled work |
    31вҶ’| Predicted Maintenance Workload | Dashed line | Shows forecasted demand before adjustment |
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’### Why Not a Pure Histogram?
    34вҶ’- RFQ shows **continuous time series** (28+ months)
    35вҶ’- Need to show **three overlapping data series**
    36вҶ’- Gap analysis requires **line comparison** not stacked bars
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’---
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’## 3. Visual Specification
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’### 3.1 Dimensions
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’```
    45вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    46вҶ’в”Ӯ  Man-Hour Projection                              [рҹ”Ҫ Timeline] в”Ӯ
    47вҶ’в”Ӯ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ  в”Ӯ
    48вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ

    52вҶ’в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   в•ұ            в•І    в•ұ       в•І                          в”Ӯ

    58вҶ’в”Ӯ       Oct Nov Dec Jan Feb Mar Apr May Jun Jul Aug Sep ...      в”Ӯ
    59вҶ’в”Ӯ       '25 '25 '25 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26          в”Ӯ
    60вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ
    61вҶ’в”Ӯ  в—Ҹ Availability  в—Ҹ Adjusted Workload  в—Ӣ Predicted Workload     в”Ӯ
    62вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    63вҶ’```
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’### 3.2 Color Palette
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’| Element | Color | Hex | Usage |
    68вҶ’|---------|-------|-----|-------|
    69вҶ’| Availability Area | Blue (muted) | `#3B82F6` opacity 30% | Capacity baseline |
    70вҶ’| Capacity Line | Blue (solid) | `#3B82F6` | Capacity threshold |
    71вҶ’| Adjusted Workload | Orange | `#F97316` | Scheduled work |
    72вҶ’| Predicted Workload | Gray dashed | `#6B7280` | Unleveled forecast |
    73вҶ’| Overallocation Zone | Red | `#EF4444` opacity 20% | Gap > 0 (demand > capacity) |

    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’### 3.3 Data Labels
    77вҶ’
    78вҶ’- Show values on line markers when hovered
    79вҶ’- Show month labels on X-axis (abbreviated: Jan, Feb, Mar...)
    80вҶ’- Show year change markers (vertical dashed line)
    81вҶ’- Y-axis: Man-hours in increments of 1000 or auto-scaled
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’---
    84вҶ’
    85вҶ’## 4. Data Table Component (Below Chart)
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’Per RFQ Section 8, a data table **must** accompany the chart:
    88вҶ’
    89вҶ’```
    90вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

    93вҶ’в”Ӯ No of Taskв”Ӯ   55  в”Ӯ   64   в”Ӯ   62   в”Ӯ   45   в”Ӯ   57   в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ
    94вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө

    96вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
    97вҶ’в”Ӯ Adj.Work в”Ӯ  3564 в”Ӯ  2754  в”Ӯ  3990  в”Ӯ  2698  в”Ӯ  2306  в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ
    98вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө

   100вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
   101вҶ’в”Ӯ Manpower в”Ӯ   19  в”Ӯ   15   в”Ӯ   22   в”Ӯ   22   в”Ӯ   22   в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ
   102вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
   103вҶ’в”Ӯ Gap(MH)  в”Ӯ   8   в”Ӯ  165   в”Ӯ -639   в”Ӯ  1031  в”Ӯ  230   в”Ӯ ...    в”Ӯ
   104вҶ’в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ  рҹҹў   в”Ӯ  рҹҹў    в”Ӯ  рҹ”ҙ    в”Ӯ  рҹҹў    в”Ӯ  рҹҹў    в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ
   105вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   106вҶ’```
   107вҶ’
   108вҶ’### Gap Row Highlighting Rules
   109вҶ’
   110вҶ’| Gap Value | Color | Icon | Meaning |
   111вҶ’|-----------|-------|------|---------|
   112вҶ’| Gap > 500 | Light Green | рҹҹў | Comfortable capacity |
   113вҶ’| 0 < Gap вүӨ 500 | Yellow | рҹҹЎ | Near capacity |
   114вҶ’| -500 вүӨ Gap < 0 | Orange | рҹҹ  | Slight overallocation |
   115вҶ’| Gap < -500 | Red | рҹ”ҙ | Critical overallocation |
   116вҶ’
   117вҶ’---
   118вҶ’
   119вҶ’## 5. Interaction Design
   120вҶ’
   121вҶ’### 5.1 Timeline Controls
   122вҶ’
   123вҶ’```
   124вҶ’[вҶҗ] [Jan 2026 в–ј] to [Dec 2027 в–ј] [вҶ’]  [1M] [3M] [6M] [1Y] [ALL]
   125вҶ’```
   126вҶ’
   127вҶ’- **Date pickers**: Start/end month selection
   128вҶ’- **Quick ranges**: 1 month, 3 months, 6 months, 1 year, All (12 years)
   129вҶ’- **Arrow navigation**: Shift view by selected range
   130вҶ’
   131вҶ’### 5.2 Hover Interactions
   132вҶ’
   133вҶ’When hovering over a data point:
   134вҶ’```
   135вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   136вҶ’в”Ӯ February 2026          в”Ӯ
   137вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
   138вҶ’в”Ӯ Availability: 3,556 MH в”Ӯ
   139вҶ’в”Ӯ Adjusted: 3,247 MH     в”Ӯ
   140вҶ’в”Ӯ Predicted: 4,102 MH    в”Ӯ
   141вҶ’в”Ӯ Gap: +309 MH           в”Ӯ
   142вҶ’в”Ӯ Utilization: 91.3%     в”Ӯ
   143вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   144вҶ’```
   145вҶ’
   146вҶ’### 5.3 Click Actions
   147вҶ’
   148вҶ’- **Click on data point**: Opens detail panel for that month
   149вҶ’- **Click on Gap cell**: Opens adjustment wizard
   150вҶ’- **Double-click**: Zoom to selected month Вұ 3 months
   151вҶ’
   152вҶ’### 5.4 Comparison Mode Toggle
   153вҶ’
   154вҶ’```
   155вҶ’[Without Adjustment] [With Adjustment] [Side-by-Side]
   156вҶ’```
   157вҶ’
   158вҶ’- **Without Adjustment**: Shows predicted workload without leveling
   159вҶ’- **With Adjustment**: Shows leveled workload (optimized)
   160вҶ’- **Side-by-Side**: Shows both charts stacked vertically
   161вҶ’
   162вҶ’---
   163вҶ’
   164вҶ’## 6. Responsive Design
   165вҶ’
   166вҶ’### Desktop (вүҘ1200px)
   167вҶ’- Full chart with data table below
   168вҶ’- All columns visible in table
   169вҶ’- Side panel for filters
   170вҶ’
   171вҶ’### Tablet (768px - 1199px)
   172вҶ’- Chart takes full width
   173вҶ’- Data table scrolls horizontally
   174вҶ’- Filters in collapsible header
   175вҶ’
   176вҶ’### Mobile (< 768px)
   177вҶ’- Simplified chart (key metrics only)
   178вҶ’- Data table replaced with card-based view
   179вҶ’- Swipe to navigate months
   180вҶ’
   181вҶ’---
   182вҶ’
   183вҶ’## 7. Accessibility
   184вҶ’
   185вҶ’### WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance
   186вҶ’
   187вҶ’| Requirement | Implementation |
   188вҶ’|-------------|----------------|
   189вҶ’| Color contrast | All text вүҘ 4.5:1 ratio |
   190вҶ’| Non-color indicators | Patterns + icons for status |
   191вҶ’| Keyboard navigation | Tab through data points |
   192вҶ’| Screen reader | ARIA labels for all elements |
   193вҶ’| Focus indicators | Visible focus ring on interactive elements |
   194вҶ’
   195вҶ’### Alternative Views
   196вҶ’
   197вҶ’- **Table-only mode**: For screen reader users
   198вҶ’- **High contrast mode**: Black/white with patterns
   199вҶ’- **Data export**: CSV/Excel for external analysis
   200вҶ’
   201вҶ’---
   202вҶ’
   203вҶ’## 8. Data Model (TypeScript)
   204вҶ’
   205вҶ’```typescript
   206вҶ’// Chart data point for a single time period
   207вҶ’interface ManHourDataPoint {
   208вҶ’  period: {
   209вҶ’    year: number;
   210вҶ’    month: number;       // 1-12
   211вҶ’    label: string;       // "Jan-26"
   212вҶ’  };
   213вҶ’  taskCount: number;
   214вҶ’  predictedWorkload: number;    // Before adjustment
   215вҶ’  adjustedWorkload: number;     // After leveling
   216вҶ’  availableManHours: number;    // Capacity
   217вҶ’  availableManpower: number;    // Headcount
   218вҶ’  gap: number;                  // available - adjusted
   219вҶ’  utilizationPercent: number;   // (adjusted / available) * 100
   220вҶ’}
   221вҶ’
   222вҶ’// Chart configuration
   223вҶ’interface ManHourChartConfig {
   224вҶ’  timeRange: {
   225вҶ’    start: { year: number; month: number };
   226вҶ’    end: { year: number; month: number };
   227вҶ’  };
   228вҶ’  comparisonMode: 'without_adjustment' | 'with_adjustment' | 'side_by_side';
   229вҶ’  fleetFilter: 'ALL' | 'KINKI_SHARYO' | 'ALSTOM';
   230вҶ’  showDataTable: boolean;
   231вҶ’  showGapHighlighting: boolean;
   232вҶ’}
   233вҶ’
   234вҶ’// Chart state for UI
   235вҶ’interface ManHourChartState {
   236вҶ’  data: ManHourDataPoint[];
   237вҶ’  config: ManHourChartConfig;
   238вҶ’  hoveredPoint: ManHourDataPoint | null;
   239вҶ’  selectedPoint: ManHourDataPoint | null;
   240вҶ’  isLoading: boolean;
   241вҶ’  error: string | null;
   242вҶ’}
   243вҶ’
   244вҶ’// Gap severity classification
   245вҶ’type GapSeverity = 'comfortable' | 'near_capacity' | 'slight_over' | 'critical_over';
   246вҶ’
   247вҶ’function classifyGap(gap: number): GapSeverity {
   248вҶ’  if (gap > 500) return 'comfortable';
   249вҶ’  if (gap > 0) return 'near_capacity';
   250вҶ’  if (gap >= -500) return 'slight_over';
   251вҶ’  return 'critical_over';
   252вҶ’}
   253вҶ’```
   254вҶ’
   255вҶ’---
   256вҶ’
   257вҶ’## 9. Component Architecture
   258вҶ’
   259вҶ’```
   260вҶ’ManHourProjectionWidget/
   261вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ ManHourChart.tsx           # Main chart (recharts/visx)
   262вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ ManHourDataTable.tsx       # Data table below chart
   263вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ TimelineControls.tsx       # Date range selector
   264вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ ComparisonToggle.tsx       # Without/With adjustment toggle
   265вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ ChartTooltip.tsx           # Hover tooltip
   266вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ GapIndicator.tsx           # Gap cell with color coding
   267вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ ManHourDetailPanel.tsx     # Side panel for month details
   268вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ hooks/
   269вҶ’    в”ңв”Җв”Җ useManHourData.ts      # Data fetching hook
   270вҶ’    в”ңв”Җв”Җ useChartConfig.ts      # Configuration state
   271вҶ’    в””в”Җв”Җ useGapAnalysis.ts      # Gap calculations
   272вҶ’```
   273вҶ’
   274вҶ’---
   275вҶ’
   276вҶ’## 10. Integration Points
   277вҶ’
   278вҶ’### Data Sources
   279вҶ’- `ResourceLevelerService.getProjection(timeRange)` - Get man-hour projections
   280вҶ’- `SimulationService.compareScenarios()` - Compare adjustment strategies
   281вҶ’
   282вҶ’### Events Emitted
   283вҶ’- `onPeriodSelect(period)` - When user selects a specific month
   284вҶ’- `onAdjustmentRequest(period)` - When user wants to adjust a month
   285вҶ’- `onExport(format)` - When user exports chart/data
   286вҶ’
   287вҶ’### Events Consumed
   288вҶ’- `maintenanceScheduleUpdated` - Refresh chart data
   289вҶ’- `resourceConfigChanged` - Recalculate availability
   290вҶ’
   291вҶ’---
   292вҶ’
   293вҶ’## 11. Performance Considerations
   294вҶ’
   295вҶ’| Scenario | Data Points | Strategy |
   296вҶ’|----------|-------------|----------|
   297вҶ’| 1 Year | 12 | Render all |
   298вҶ’| 5 Years | 60 | Render all |
   299вҶ’| 12 Years | 144 | Virtualize table, aggregate chart |
   300вҶ’
   301вҶ’### Optimization Strategies
   302вҶ’1. **Lazy load data table rows** beyond viewport
   303вҶ’2. **Aggregate to quarters** for views > 3 years
   304вҶ’3. **Debounce hover events** (100ms)
   305вҶ’4. **Memoize gap calculations**
   306вҶ’
   307вҶ’---
   308вҶ’
   309вҶ’## 12. Unknown Unknowns Discovered
   310вҶ’
   311вҶ’Through competitor research, several risks identified:
   312вҶ’
   313вҶ’| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
   314вҶ’|------|--------|------------|
   315вҶ’| **Timezone issues** | Man-hours span midnight | Store in UTC, display in local |
   316вҶ’| **Holiday calendars** | Availability varies by country | Configurable working days |
   317вҶ’| **Overtime calculation** | Gap doesn't account for OT | Add "max overtime" parameter |
   318вҶ’| **Skill mismatch** | Man-hours available вү  qualified | Add skill-based filtering |
   319вҶ’| **Multi-depot planning** | Each depot has different capacity | Depot-level breakdown view |
   320вҶ’| **Emergency maintenance** | Unplanned work disrupts schedule | Reserve buffer capacity |
   321вҶ’
   322вҶ’---
   323вҶ’
   324вҶ’## 13. Sources & References
   325вҶ’
   326вҶ’- [Resource Histogram Best Practices - ProjectManager.com](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/what-is-a-resource-histogram)
   327вҶ’- [Capacity Planning Software - The Digital Project Manager](https://thedigitalprojectmanager.com/tools/best-capacity-planning-software/)
   328вҶ’- [Choosing Chart Types - Nielsen Norman Group](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/choosing-chart-types/)
   329вҶ’- [Data Visualization Handbook - UX Magazine](https://uxmag.com/articles/the-ultimate-data-visualization-handbook-for-designers)
   330вҶ’- [Stacked Bar Charts - Domo](https://www.domo.com/learn/charts/stacked-bar-charts)
   331вҶ’- [Workload Management Tools - Runn](https://www.runn.io/blog/workload-management-tools)
   332вҶ’
   333вҶ’---
   334вҶ’
   335вҶ’## 14. Approval Checklist
   336вҶ’
   337вҶ’- [ ] Reviewed by UX Designer
   338вҶ’- [ ] Reviewed by Product Owner
   339вҶ’- [ ] RFQ Section 8 compliance verified
   340вҶ’- [ ] Accessibility audit passed
   341вҶ’- [ ] Performance benchmarks met
   342вҶ’- [ ] Approved for implementation
   343вҶ’

# Man-Hour Projection Chart - UX Design Specification

**Task ID:** MILEAGE-UX-002
**Status:** CRITICAL - RFQ Section 8 Case Study
**Created:** 2026-01-08

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Man-Hour Projection Chart is the **core visualization** for resource planning in the Rolling Stock Mileage Management System. It displays workload (man-hours required) vs capacity (man-hours available) over a 12-year contract period, enabling planners to:

1. Identify resource overallocation/underallocation
2. Compare "Without adjustment" vs "With adjustment" scenarios
3. Make data-driven decisions about maintenance scheduling

**RFQ Reference:** Section 8 - Case Studies (Page 4)

---

## 2. Chart Type Selection

### Primary Chart: Stacked Area/Line Combination Chart

Based on RFQ Section 8 and [industry best practices](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/what-is-a-resource-histogram):

| Chart Element | Type | Purpose |
|---------------|------|---------|
| Availability (Capacity) | Solid Area | Shows available man-hours as baseline |
| Adjusted Workload | Line with markers | Shows scheduled/leveled work |
| Predicted Maintenance Workload | Dashed line | Shows forecasted demand before adjustment |

### Why Not a Pure Histogram?
- RFQ shows **continuous time series** (28+ months)
- Need to show **three overlapping data series**
- Gap analysis requires **line comparison** not stacked bars

---

## 3. Visual Specification

### 3.1 Dimensions

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  Man-Hour Projection                              [рҹ”Ҫ Timeline] в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  8000 в”Ө                                                         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ         в•ӯв”Җв”Җв•®                                            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  6000 в”Ө    в•ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв•Ҝ  в•°в”Җв”Җв”Җв•®      в•ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•®                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   в•ұ            в•І    в•ұ       в•І                          в”Ӯ

```

### 3.2 Color Palette

| Element | Color | Hex | Usage |
|---------|-------|-----|-------|
| Availability Area | Blue (muted) | `#3B82F6` opacity 30% | Capacity baseline |
| Capacity Line | Blue (solid) | `#3B82F6` | Capacity threshold |
| Adjusted Workload | Orange | `#F97316` | Scheduled work |
| Predicted Workload | Gray dashed | `#6B7280` | Unleveled forecast |
| Overallocation Zone | Red | `#EF4444` opacity 20% | Gap > 0 (demand > capacity) |
| Underallocation Zone | Green | `#22C55E` opacity 20% | Gap < 0 (capacity > demand) |

### 3.3 Data Labels

- Show values on line markers when hovered
- Show month labels on X-axis (abbreviated: Jan, Feb, Mar...)
- Show year change markers (vertical dashed line)
- Y-axis: Man-hours in increments of 1000 or auto-scaled

---

## 4. Data Table Component (Below Chart)

Per RFQ Section 8, a data table **must** accompany the chart:

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

в”Ӯ No of Taskв”Ӯ   55  в”Ӯ   64   в”Ӯ   62   в”Ӯ   45   в”Ӯ   57   в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ Maint.Workв”Ӯ  3706 в”Ӯ  3206  в”Ӯ  4201  в”Ӯ  2033  в”Ӯ  3042  в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ Adj.Work в”Ӯ  3564 в”Ӯ  2754  в”Ӯ  3990  в”Ӯ  2698  в”Ӯ  2306  в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Avail(MH)в”Ӯ  3576 в”Ӯ  2929  в”Ӯ  3351  в”Ӯ  3729  в”Ӯ  2536  в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Manpower в”Ӯ   19  в”Ӯ   15   в”Ӯ   22   в”Ӯ   22   в”Ӯ   22   в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ
